factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across segments, with some showing growth and others decline.

evidence.text: Aerospace sales increased in the quarter ended September 30, 2017 primarily due to organic sales growth and favorable impact of foreign currency translation, partially offset by a decrease due to the government services business divestiture. Aerospace sales decreased in the nine months ended September 30, 2017 primarily due to the government services business divestiture, partially offset by an increase in organic sales growth.
evidence.subsection: Aerospace

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand trends with growth in some segments and decline in others.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill information is not explicitly provided in the text.

evidence.text: No specific mention of backlog orders or book-to-bill ratios.
evidence.subsection: (Dollars in millions, except per share amounts)

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No specific information available on backlog orders or book-to-bill ratios.

factors.summary: The company is successfully holding prices in most segments, with some segments showing slight increases.

evidence.text: The effective tax rate decreased for the quarter primarily driven by increased tax benefits from foreign tax credits and the absence of divestitures taxed at higher rates, partially offset by increased expense for reserves and decreased tax benefits for employee share-based payments.
evidence.subsection: (Dollars in millions, except per share amounts)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Company is successfully holding prices and some segments show slight increases.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in Aerospace and Performance Materials.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased in the quarter primarily due to higher gross margin in Aerospace and Performance Materials and Technologies (approximately 2.5 percentage point impact collectively) and lower repositioning and other charges allocated to cost of products and services sold (approximately 0.3 percentage point impact), partially offset by lower gross margin in Home and Building Technologies and Safety and Productivity Solutions (approximately 0.4 percentage point impact collectively).
evidence.subsection: (Dollars in millions, except per share amounts)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable to slightly increasing in most segments, with some exceptions due to divestitures.

evidence.text: Organic growth/ Operational segment profit increased in the quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2017 primarily due to organic sales growth.
evidence.subsection: (Dollars in millions, except per share amounts)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is stable to slightly increasing in most segments.

factors.summary: Volume trends are growing in most segments, driven by increased sales and production.

evidence.text: Cost of products and services sold decreased in the quarter primarily due to lower labor costs of approximately $110 million (driven primarily by divestitures, net of acquisitions), and decreased indirect material costs of approximately $90 million (driven primarily by productivity, net of inflation), and lower repositioning and other charges of approximately $30 million, partially offset by higher organic sales volumes that drove increased direct materials of approximately $220 million.
evidence.subsection: (Dollars in millions, except per share amounts)

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends are growing in most segments due to increased sales and production.

evidence.text: Aerospace sales increased in the quarter ended September 30, 2017 primarily due to organic sales growth and favorable impact of foreign currency translation, partially offset by a decrease due to the government services business divestiture.
evidence.subsection: Aerospace

sentiment.label: neutral

evidence.text: Cost of products and services sold decreased in the quarter primarily due to lower labor costs of approximately $110 million (driven primarily by divestitures, net of acquisitions), and decreased indirect material costs of approximately $90 million (driven primarily by productivity, net of inflation), and lower repositioning and other charges of approximately $30 million, partially offset by higher organic sales volumes that drove increased direct materials of approximately $220 million.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving, driven by higher gross margins and productivity gains.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased in the quarter primarily due to higher gross margin in Aerospace and Performance Materials and Technologies (approximately 2.5 percentage point impact collectively) and lower repositioning and other charges allocated to cost of products and services sold (approximately 0.3 percentage point impact), partially offset by lower gross margin in Home and Building Technologies and Safety and Productivity Solutions (approximately 0.4 percentage point impact collectively).

sentiment.rationale: Improving gross and operating margins driven by higher gross margins and productivity gains.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, with repositioning actions generating incremental savings.

evidence.text: Our repositioning actions are expected to generate incremental pretax savings of $350 million to $400 million in 2017 compared with 2016 primarily from planned workforce reductions. Cash spending related to our repositioning actions was $123 million in the nine months ended September 30, 2017 and was funded through operating cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Repositioning and Other Charges

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant savings from cost reduction programs, particularly workforce reductions, show effective cost management.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by restructuring benefits and efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: The increase in operational segment profit was driven primarily by productivity including restructuring benefits, lower OEM incentives and higher organic sales volume, partially offset by inflation and the government services business divestiture. Cost of products and services sold decreased primarily due to the government services business divestiture, and productivity, net of inflation, including restructuring benefits.
evidence.subsection: Aerospace

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements from restructuring and efficiency initiatives contribute to higher operational profit.

factors.summary: Management expects continued growth, with a projected effective tax rate of approximately 22% for 2017.

evidence.text: The Company currently expects the effective tax rate for 2017 to be approximately 22%. The effective tax rate can vary from quarter to quarter for unusual or infrequently occurring items, the resolution of income tax audits, changes in tax laws or other items such as pension mark-to-market adjustments and the tax impact from employee share-based payments.
evidence.subsection: (Dollars in millions, except per share amounts)

factors.summary: Management is confident, as indicated by increased dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: Effective with the fourth quarter 2017 dividend, we increased our quarterly dividend rate by 12% to $0.745 per share of common stock. The Company repurchased $343 million of outstanding shares... Honeywell presently expects to repurchase outstanding shares from time to time to offset the dilutive impact over the long-term of employee stock-based compensation plans...
evidence.subsection: Liquidity

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on core business growth and selective divestitures.

evidence.text: The Company continues to manage its businesses to maximize operating cash flows as the primary source of liquidity. In addition to our available cash and operating cash flows, additional sources of liquidity include committed credit lines, short-term debt from the commercial paper market, long-term borrowings, as well as access to the public debt and equity markets.
evidence.subsection: Liquidity

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks primarily related to proposed spin-offs of its Homes and Global Distribution business and Transportation Systems business. These risks include potential integration challenges, complexities in separation processes, and uncertainties regarding tax implications and regulatory approvals. The spin-offs are contingent on various conditions, may not be completed on time or at all, and could fail to achieve the intended benefits.

evidence.text: The proposed spin-offs of our Homes and Global Distribution business and of our Transportation Systems business into two stand-alone, publicly-traded companies are each contingent upon the satisfaction of a number of conditions, may not be completed on the currently contemplated timeline, or at all, and may not achieve the intended benefits.
evidence.subsection: Risk Factors

evidence.text: Completion of each proposed spin-off is subject to finalization of the financial statements of the spun-off business, assurance that the separation will be tax-free to Honeywell shareowners for U.S. federal income tax purposes, finalization of the capital structure of the three corporations, the effectiveness of appropriate filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, final approval of the Honeywell Board of Directors, and other customary matters. Each proposed spin-off is complex in nature, and may be affected by unanticipated developments, credit and equity markets, or changes in market conditions.
evidence.subsection: (Dollars in millions, except per share amounts)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks related to proposed spin-offs include integration challenges and uncertainties.
